title: Market Analyst JSON Conventions
description: All JSON files in the market-analyst pipeline MUST follow these conventions to prevent data parsing errors during analysis.

encoding.description: All files use UTF-8 encoding. BOM is included for Windows compatibility.

rules: Always include timezone offset, never use 'Z' shorthand — use '+00:00' instead
Never use Unix timestamps
Date-only fields use 'YYYY-MM-DD' format

decimal_safe_strings.description: Fields representing monetary values, prices, percentages, or any value where floating-point precision matters MUST use string type with exact decimal representation.

rules: Use explicit null for missing or unavailable data
Never use empty string '' as a substitute for null
Never use 0 or -1 as sentinel values for missing data
Never omit keys — include the key with null value
Always use arrays even for single items — no scalar/array ambiguity
Empty collections are represented as [] not null
Array items must all share the same schema — no mixed types
Enum values are lowercase_snake_case strings
Invalid enum values must be rejected at parse time
New enum values require schema_version bump
Every root JSON object includes 'schema_version': '1.0'
Backward-incompatible changes require major version bump
Parsers must check schema_version before processing
Every fetch or process result includes a 'status' field
'ok' — all data retrieved/processed successfully
'partial' — some data available but incomplete
'error' — failed entirely, check 'error_message' field
Ticker symbols are UPPERCASE (e.g., 'TSMC', 'AAPL')
Source IDs are lowercase_snake_case (e.g., 'yfinance', 'alpha_vantage')
Report IDs use format: '{report_type}_{YYYYMMDD}_{HHmmss}'
